steps.title: Write lesson: {name}
steps.description: Write {path} for a coder with LOW experience: friendly, zero-assumed-knowledge, every concept introduced before use, every term glossary-linked on first use. Structure: why this matters (2-3 sentences), the idea (plain words + one analogy), try it (commands against tools/evals/fixtures/portfolio with expected output), what you just did, where to go next. Warm, nerdy, encouraging tone — 'made with love for creators of all kind' is the voice of this lane.
acceptance_criteria: try-it commands target the fixture portfolio

steps.title: Verify every try-it block in {name}
steps.description: Run each fenced command in {path} against the fixture portfolio; fix drift; register the lesson in the doc-journey runner so CI re-verifies it.
acceptance_criteria: all blocks execute with the documented output
lesson registered in the journey runner

steps.title: Beginner-lens review of {name}
steps.description: Re-read {path} as someone who has never used a terminal beyond basics: flag and fix every leap of logic, missing prerequisite, or intimidating phrasing. Add a 'stuck? this is normal' note where failure is likely.
acceptance_criteria: at least one reassurance note at the likeliest failure point

steps.title: Self-check for {name}
steps.description: Append 3 self-check questions with hidden (details-tag) answers that test understanding, not memory.

steps.title: Wire {name} into the intro-lane path
steps.description: Prev/next links + entry in docs/intro/START_HERE.md index; the whole lane must be walkable start to finish without dead ends.
